steps.title: Identify the comparison
steps.content: The underlined phrase uses "much like" to compare the mantis filters to the filters in 3D movie glasses. That familiar comparison helps readers understand the study's experimental setup.

steps.title: Reject wrong functions
steps.content: Choice A treats the phrase as an earlier use of the same tool. Choice B invents a research problem. Choice C says the phrase emphasizes a difference between studies. The remaining choice identifies the phrase as a comparison for understanding. The answer is D.
